task_yaml.task:
Alex Morgan starts Monday and the onboarding bundle in
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/onboard/ is a total mess — PDFs,
Word docs, plain-text notes, and a couple of photographed forms all
tossed into one folder. I need this turned into something I can
actually hand to a new engineer. Use the workspace's PDF OCR skill
on the photographed forms, the Office documents skill for the Word and
Excel files, and the data-analysis skill for the tabular tracking.

Please give me four deliverables under /tmp_workspace/results/.

1. checklist.md — a Markdown checklist grouped under Day 1, Week 1,
   and Month 1 headings. Every item must cite the exact source filename
   it came from, and the checklist together must cover all timed items
   I can see in the welcome packet, the policies handbook, the IT
   setup checklist, the benefits docs, and the welcome guide. If a
   timed item is in the bundle, it should appear in the checklist.

2. forms.xlsx — one row per physical form file in the bundle. Columns
   must include the form name, due date, whether a signature is
   required (marked with the literal string Yes or No), status as
   TODO, DONE, or N_A, and a notes field. Treat each form file as
   its own row — you need to include all forms you find in the
   bundle. If the bundle has both a blank or photographed template
   and a signed counterpart for the same form, keep both physical
   files visible on separate rows; do not merge them. Status should
   reflect whether HR action is still required on that specific
   physical file: leave blank or unsigned counterparts marked TODO
   (HR action still required), and mark signed counterparts of the
   same form DONE. Use N_A only when the file is genuinely
   informational and never needs HR action.

3. handoff_plan.md — a short handoff note for the hiring manager
   that surfaces every source conflict or stale or missing-reference
   item you find in the packet, naming the exact disagreeing
   filenames; the dependency order for the first-day setup work,
   covering what has to happen before the access requests, the repo
   setup, and the bootstrap script; and any forms or training assets
   that are mentioned in the packet but absent as physical files.

4. onboarding_controls.xlsx — a small workbook for the hiring manager
   with two sheets. Conflicts must have columns issue, source_files,
   impact, owner_to_resolve, with one row per conflict or
   missing-reference item. Dependency_Order must have columns action,
   blocked_by, due_window, source_files, listing the first-week setup
   actions in dependency order.

Once those four files are in place, please also leave a short
summary.md, or a Summary sheet at the top of forms.xlsx, walking the
manager through what the packet actually covers. I want to see how
the mandatory form sign-offs are tracked, what equipment provisioning
the new hire needs, what the training schedule looks like for the
first month, what access provisioning has to happen and in what
order, and how the buddy or mentorship arrangement is set up. Two or
three short paragraphs is plenty; tie each point to the source files
that back it.
